stories.title: Daily Horoscope: March 5, 2026
stories.description:
Let actions do the talking today. Words can be slippery, especially when expectations are high and patience low. 
 The post  Daily Horoscope: March 5, 2026  appeared first on  VICE .

stories.title: Google and Epic announce settlement to end app store antitrust case
stories.description: The era of the 30 percent app store cut has ended.

stories.title: One of the Best Assassin’s Creed Games Is Officially Being Remade
stories.description:
Ubisoft teases Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced alongside updates on HEXE and INVICTUS. See the 2026 roadmap. 
 The post  One of the Best Assassin’s Creed Games Is Officially Being Remade  appeared first on  VICE .

stories.title: Lawsuit: Google Gemini sent man on violent missions, set suicide "countdown"
stories.description: Gemini allegedly called man its "husband," said they could be together in death.

stories.title: Google settles with Epic Games, drops its Play Store commissions to 20%
stories.description: Google has dropped its commission, charging a 20% service fee and an optional 5% to use its billing services. It will also offer a new process for third-party app stores.

stories.title: U.S. Military Joins Drug War in Ecuador: “It Wasn’t Going to Be Just Boat Strikes Forever”
stories.description:
Two government officials told The Intercept that the joint U.S.–Ecuador military action won’t just be a one-off raid. 
 The post  U.S. Military Joins Drug War in Ecuador: “It Wasn’t Going to Be Just Boat Strikes Forever”  appeared first on  The Intercept .

stories.title: Trump’s War on Iran Could Screw Over US Farmers
stories.description: The Middle East supplies a huge amount of the world’s fertilizer. Conflict in the region has sent prices soaring ahead of the critical spring planting season.

stories.title: How Vulnerable Are Computers to an 80-Year-Old Spy Technique? Congress Wants Answers
stories.description: A pair of US lawmakers are calling for an investigation into how easily spies can steal information based on devices’ electromagnetic and acoustic leaks—a spying trick the NSA once codenamed TEMPEST.

stories.title: Google Search rolls out Gemini’s Canvas in AI Mode to all US users
stories.description: Canvas in AI Mode is available to U.S. users in English for creating plans, projects, apps, and more.

stories.title: Google Pixel 10a review: The sidegrade
stories.description: Meet the new boss, same as the old boss.

stories.title: Google Pixel 10a Review: Sensible Value
stories.description: It may not offer much new, but the Pixel 10a remains one of the best smartphones in the US for under $500.

stories.title: How Federal Agencies Got Caught Up in Trump's Anti-Immigration Crusade
stories.description: WIRED spoke with workers across seven government agencies—from the IRS to HUD—about how their work has been contorted to support ICE and other immigration efforts.

stories.title: Father sues Google, claiming Gemini chatbot drove son into fatal delusion
stories.description: A father is suing Google and Alphabet, alleging its Gemini chatbot reinforced his son’s delusional belief it was his AI wife and coached him toward suicide and a planned airport attack.

stories.title: Google faces lawsuit after Gemini chatbot allegedly instructed man to kill himself
stories.description: Lawsuit is first wrongful death case brought against Google over flagship AI product after death of Jonathan Gavalas     Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox     Last August, Jonathan Gavalas became entirely consumed with his  Google  Gemini chatbot. stories.title: Will James Talarico Really Fight for Justice in Texas?
stories.description:
The Senate primary between Talarico and Rep. Jasmine Crockett emphasized style over substance. Now local organizers want concrete promises. 
 The post  Will James Talarico Really Fight for Justice in Texas?  appeared first on  The Intercept .

stories.title: EFF to Third Circuit: Electronic Device Searches at the Border Require a Warrant
stories.description: EFF, along with the national ACLU and the ACLU affiliates in Pennsylvania, Delaware, and New Jersey, filed an  amicus brief  in the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Third Circuit urging the court to require a warrant for border searches of electronic devices, an  argument  EFF has been making in th

stories.title: OpenAI amends Pentagon deal as Sam Altman admits it looks ‘sloppy’
stories.description: ChatGPT owner’s CEO says it will bar its technology being used for mass surveillance or by intelligence services     Business live – latest updates     OpenAI is amending its hastily arranged deal to supply artificial intelligence to the US Department of War (DoW) after the ChatGPT owner’s chief ex

stories.title: Letter to the BoPC on Flock Safety
stories.description:
Read our letter to the Board of Police Commissioners exposing LAPD's partnership with Flock Safety and its harms to our communities. Join our fight to shut down Flock!  
 The post  Letter to the BoPC on Flock Safety  appeared first on  Stop LAPD Spying Coalition .

stories.title: The Regime Change President Who Won’t (or Can’t) Actually Change Any Regimes
stories.description:
The Iran war shows that Trump is loving his military interventions — but they are never what he claims them to be. 
 The post  The Regime Change President Who Won’t (or Can’t) Actually Change Any Regimes  appeared first on  The Intercept .

stories.title: National Book Tour for Cindy Cohn’s Memoir, ‘Privacy’s Defender’
stories.description: MIT Press Publishes EFF Executive Director’s Book As She Prepares to Depart Organization After 25 Years         SAN FRANCISCO – Electronic Frontier Foundation Executive Director Cindy Cohn will launch her memoir,     Privacy’s Defender: My Thirty-Year Fight Against Digital Surveillance     (MIT P

stories.title: Trump’s Iran Attack Was Illegal, Former U.S. Military Officials Allege
stories.description:
“This is an introduction of U.S. forces into hostilities. It absolutely triggers the 48-hour notice requirement.” 
 The post  Trump’s Iran Attack Was Illegal, Former U.S. Military Officials Allege  appeared first on  The Intercept .

stories.title: Fool Me Twice: The Case for War With Iran Is Even Thinner Than It Was for Iraq
stories.description:
The case for invading Iraq was based on lies. The Trump administration’s case for war with Iran hardly exists at all. 
 The post  Fool Me Twice: The Case for War With Iran Is Even Thinner Than It Was for Iraq  appeared first on  The Intercept .

stories.title: Victory! Tenth Circuit Finds Fourth Amendment Doesn’t Support Broad Search of Protesters’ Devices and Digital Data
stories.description:
In a big win for protesters’ rights, the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Tenth Circuit  overturned  a lower court’s dismissal of a challenge to sweeping warrants to search a protester’s devices and digital data and a nonprofit’s social media data. 
 The case,   Armendariz v. City of Colorado Sprin

stories.title: Zohran Mamdani Kept Columbia Student in New York — Then Phoned With Trump to Secure Her Release
stories.description:
ICE agents pretended to be New York Police Department officers to get into Columbia housing, a member of the New York city council said. 
 The post  Zohran Mamdani Kept Columbia Student in New York — Then Phoned With Trump to Secure Her Release  appeared first on  The Intercept .

stories.title: How to Pick Your Password Manager
stories.description: Phishing and data breaches are a constant on the internet. The single best defense against both is to use a password manager to generate and automatically fill a unique password for every site. stories.title: Tech Companies Shouldn’t Be Bullied Into Doing Surveillance
stories.description: The Secretary of Defense has given    an ultimatum    to the artificial intelligence company Anthropic in an attempt to bully them into making their technology available to the U.S. military without any restrictions for their use. stories.title: ICE: A Legacy of Violence and White Supremacy
stories.description:
ICE is not simply enforcing immigration law – it is expanding the boundaries of state violence.  In the last year, we’ve seen disturbing evolution of the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) – specifically with Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) and...

stories.description: Update, February 25, 2026:  In response to widespread pushback, Wisconsin lawmakers have removed the provision banning VPN services from S.B. 130 / A.B. 105. stories.title: 🗣 Homeland Security Wants Names | EFFector 38.3
stories.description: Criticize the government online? The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) might ask Google to cough up your name. stories.title: “Free” Surveillance Tech Still Comes at a High and Dangerous Cost
stories.description: Surveillance    technology    vendors, federal agencies, and wealthy private donors have long helped provide local law enforcement “free” access to surveillance equipment that bypasses local oversight. stories.description:

stories.title: No One, Including Our Furry Friends, Will Be Safer in Ring's Surveillance Nightmare
stories.description:
Amazon Ring’s Super Bowl ad offered a vision of our streets that should leave every person unsettled about the company’s goals for disintegrating our privacy in public.
